{{Quote|Don't make me do it!|Kar Yang's last words to Guri|<ref name="SOTEE3" />}}
'''Kar Yang''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Hiitian/Legends|Hiitian]], considered to be the second-most skilled and feared [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. He was often accompanied by [[Lintu]], his assistant [[Droid/Legends|droid]]. He came to prominence after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and hunted [[Guri]].

==Biography==
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of the [[Hiitian/Legends|Hiitian]]<ref name="SOTEE1" /> [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|42|Hiitian}}</ref> Kar Yang was [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] second-most talented and renowned [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]<ref name="SOTEE1" /> by [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="Date" /> Possessed of greater skill as a [[Gunslinger/Legends|gunslinger]] than the [[Chadra-Fan/Legends|Chadra-Fan]] [[Tutti Snibit]], the legendary Yang nonetheless relied upon his fellow hunter as a source of information on multiple occasions.<ref name="TNSM7">{{SW|url=news/the-not-so-magnificent-seven|text=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|int=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|nolive=1}}</ref>

Yang was hired by the [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] [[Antiques dealer/Legends|antiques dealer]] [[Savan|Azool]] to capture the [[Human replica droid]] [[Guri]], who carried sensitive information on Black Sun that could grant control of the syndicate to their possessor.<ref name="Guri">{{DB|droid|guri|Guri}}</ref> Azool was in reality [[Savan]], the niece of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] crime syndicate's [[Death/Legends|deceased]] [[Underlord]] [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]]. Savan provided the hunter with information on Guri's whereabouts provided by the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Prevaro]].<ref name="SOTEE1" /> Savan covertly observed Yang through his personal [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Lintu]].<ref name="SOTEE3" />

Aboard his [[Kar Yang's starship|starship]], the Hiitian tracked his quarry to the site of her [[Destruction of an Imperial II-class Star Destroyer|destruction of an ''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. Impressed, he continued on the trail of Guri, who had next visited a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] on [[Hurd's Moon]] to enlist its proprietor, her creator [[Massad Thrumble]], in removing her programming as an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]].<ref name="SOTEE1" />

Yang tracked down Thrumble, who initially refused to betray Guri's location, setting the cantina's other occupants against the hunter when threatened. Dispatching his attackers, Yang persuaded Thrumble to reveal her presence on the sparsely populated [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Murninkam]], home to rogue [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Spinda Caveel]]. On his way there, he reported his progress to Azool. [[Starfighter combat/Legends|Fighting]] his way through Caveel's [[robotic picket ship]] defenses and evading his subordinates the [[Pike Sisters|Pikkel Sisters]], Yang observed from a distance as Guri infiltrated the the scientist's fortress<ref name="SOTEE2">[[Shadows of the Empire: Evolution 2|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'' 2]]</ref> and retrieved [[A-OIC]], a specialized [[Medical droid/Legends|medical]] stolen from Thrumble by Caveel. Just before Guri left the planet, Yang tagged her [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the ''[[Stinger]]'', with a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing device]].<ref name="SOTEE3" />

Having successfully located Guri, he explained to Lintu that he had no intention of returning her to his employer. Guri was much more valuable than the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] he had been offered. Yang planned to keep Guri for his own purposes. Lintu waited until he believed Yang had gone to sleep, then reported back to Savan via comm. But Yang had grown suspicious of his droid and overheard the conversation. Yang and Lintu tracked the ''Stinger'' to Hurd's Moon. After touching down, Yang sent the droid ahead to scout and shot him in the back.<ref name="SOTEE3" />

At last, Yang came upon his quarry in Thrumble's hidden laboratory deep within Hurd's Moon. Guri had been prepared for a deep neural modification, though Yang forced her up from the operating table at gunpoint. In Yang's brief custody, he forced Guri to tie up Thrumble, and then led her to the lab exit. Guri managed to knock the blaster from Yang's grip, and the two engaged in hand-to-hand combat. Yang was able to recover his blaster from the floor and took a shot at Guri, though only grazed her leg. Once again, Yang had his quarry at gunpoint, though did not fire as he wanted her alive. Guri took advantage of his hesitance and punched him in the chest,<ref name="SOTEE3" /> tearing his heart out with her bare hand.<ref name="FF" />

The droid A-OIC dragged Yang's bloodied carcass away while Thrumble and Guri were forced to recalibrate the medical instruments thanks to Yang's interruption.<ref name="SOTEE3" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kar Yang first appeared in the [[Shadows of the Empire: Evolution 1|initial issue]] of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire: Evolution|''Shadows of the Empire: Evolution'']], written by [[Steve Perry]], illustrated by [[Ron Randall]],<ref name="SOTEE1" /> and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[February 11]]th, [[1998]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54549/star_wars_shadows_of_the_empire_-_evolution_1998_1|text=Star Wars: Shadows Of The Empire - Evolution (1998) #1}}</ref>
